parsed_review.summary: CP00-533 is a descriptor-only RP17 AI-Governance pack (P05 workflow permission slice) covering 40 units RP17.P05.M04.S13-RP17.P05.M06.S08. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim (production_ready:false, descriptor-only, no real data, no product-state writes) is fully supported. Distribution matches across four sources: registry binding (registry.js:324/1718), closeout-pack-plan.json:407, manifest.json, and review-baseline.json — unit_count 40, deliverables {implementation:14, fixture:14, test:6, hermes_evidence:2, claude_review:2, security_audit:2}, micro rows {M04:10, M05:22, M06:8}. Implementation is complete and consistent: service.js:2469-2540 produces the case set and descriptor by extending the CP532 upstream and stamping freezeCp533Result with all runtime/write/leak flags false (no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, dispatches_*_runtime:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false); validators.js:3521-3680 supplies all seven required public exports (coverage, descriptor, Hermes evidence, Claude review, closeout handoff) with descriptor_only/runtime_execution/customer_safe/raw_payload guards plus explicit assertions that Claude is not promoted to final approval and local validation does not claim enterprise trust. The contract (contracts/ai-governance-core-contract.json:10-31, cp533_requirements, cp533_no_write_attestation, descriptor projection) is regenerated to match the registry exactly, and the RP17 validator script plus model.test.js (3 dedicated CP533 blocks at lines 1302-1352) assert coverage.valid, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, and the no-write attestation. command-evidence.json records all implementation/test/contract/plan/repo validation commands at exit 0, Hermes gate H17 passed, with post-review gated commands correctly deferred to commit time. Authority boundaries hold: Claude remains a read-only reviewer (allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob, read_only:true, claude_is_final_approval:false), Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review-packet/receipt handling (not product runtime), and human final approval / enterprise-trust closure remain outside this receipt. Review-hardening evidence is clean: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1446-byte stdout, well-formed JSON), no 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output. Continuity is preserved (CP532.next_subphase RP17.P05.M04.S13 == CP533.first_unit; CP533 hands off to CP00-534 at RP17.P05.M06.S09). Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room UI, workbook docs) are correctly preserved and excluded from pack scope. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one P3 cosmetic note on the review tooling's scope-classification file, non-blocking.

findings.title: changed-file-scope double-categorizes the pack's own untracked manifest directory as unrelated
findings.details: untracked_implementation_files lists both docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-533/, yet the same two paths also appear in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 156-168). docs/closeout-packs/cp00-533/ is this pack's own generated manifest/command-evidence directory and is genuinely related to CP00-533, so labeling it 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved' is a misnomer. This is review-tooling metadata only; active_pack_implementation_refs (the tracked, modified source files) and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted ([]) are correct, so pack scope and the diff under review are unaffected.
findings.recommendation: In the scope-builder, stop classifying the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack_id>/ output directory under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (or add a distinct 'pack_generated_artifacts' bucket) to avoid the contradictory dual-listing. No change required to CP00-533 itself.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_informational: tooling-metadata cosmetic only; no impact on pack correctness, scope, boundaries, or evidence — pack may proceed to closeout
